So that the third-party platforms such as NFT market could timely update the images and related attributes of the NFT."},"Paused(address)":{"details":"Emitted when the pause is triggered by `account`."},"RoleAdminChanged(bytes32,bytes32,bytes32)":{"details":"Emitted when `newAdminRole` is set as ``role``'s admin role, replacing `previousAdminRole` `DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE` is the starting admin for all roles, despite {RoleAdminChanged} not being emitted to signal this."},"RoleGranted(bytes32,address,address)":{"details":"Emitted when `account` is granted `role`. `sender` is the account that originated the contract call. The approval is cleared when the token is transferred. Only a single account can be approved at a time, so approving the zero address clears previous approvals. Requirements: - The caller must own the token or be an approved operator. - `tokenId` must exist. Emits an {Approval} event."},"balanceOf(address)":{"details":"Returns the number of tokens in ``owner``'s account."},"constructor":{"params":{"baseIPFS":"Pr\u00e9fixe IPFS de base (ex: \"ipfs://\")","initialAdmin":"Adresse admin principale (multisig RIMOWA recommand\u00e9)","royaltyFeeBps":"Pourcentage de royalties en basis points (ex: 500 = 5%)","royaltyReceiver":"Adresse recevant les royalties (tr\u00e9sorerie RIMOWA)"}},"getApproved(uint256)":{"details":"Returns the account approved for `tokenId` token. Requirements: - `tokenId` must exist."},"getRoleAdmin(bytes32)":{"details":"Returns the admin role that controls `role`. See {grantRole} and {revokeRole}. To change a role's admin, use {_setRoleAdmin}."},"getSerialByTokenId(uint256)":{"params":{"tokenId":"Identifiant du token ERC-721"}},"getTokenIdBySerial(string)":{"params":{"serialNumber":"Num\u00e9ro de s\u00e9rie grav\u00e9 sur la valise physique"}},"grantRole(bytes32,address)":{"details":"Grants `role` to `account`. If `account` had not been already granted `role`, emits a {RoleGranted} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleGranted} event."},"hasRole(bytes32,address)":{"details":"Returns `true` if `account` has been granted `role`."},"isApprovedForAll(address,address)":{"details":"Returns if the `operator` is allowed to manage all of the assets of `owner`. See {setApprovalForAll}"},"isPassportValid(uint256)":{"params":{"tokenId":"Identifiant du token ERC-721"}},"mintPassport(address,string,string)":{"details":"Seul un MINTER_ROLE peut appeler cette fonction.      Le num\u00e9ro de s\u00e9rie doit \u00eatre unique \u2014 une valise = un token.      Le CID IPFS doit pointer vers les m\u00e9tadonn\u00e9es JSON Pinata.      Aucune donn\u00e9e brute n'est stock\u00e9e ici, seulement le CID.","params":{"ipfsCid":"CID IPFS du JSON de m\u00e9tadonn\u00e9es (sans pr\u00e9fixe)","serialNumber":"Num\u00e9ro de s\u00e9rie grav\u00e9 sur la valise physique","to":"Adresse du Smart Account propri\u00e9taire (ERC-4337)"}},"name()":{"details":"Returns the token collection name."},"ownerOf(uint256)":{"details":"Returns the owner of the `tokenId` token. Requirements: - `tokenId` must exist."},"pause()":{"details":"Utilis\u00e9 en cas de faille de s\u00e9curit\u00e9 d\u00e9tect\u00e9e."},"paused()":{"details":"Returns true if the contract is paused, and false otherwise."},"renounceRole(bytes32,address)":{"details":"Revokes `role` from the calling account. Roles are often managed via {grantRole} and {revokeRole}: this function's purpose is to provide a mechanism for accounts to lose their privileges if they are compromised (such as when a trusted device is misplaced). If the calling account had been revoked `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must be `callerConfirmation`. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event."},"revokePassport(uint256,string)":{"details":"Seul un REVOKER_ROLE peut r\u00e9voquer. Le token reste dans le mapping      mais est marqu\u00e9 comme invalide. Les transferts sont bloqu\u00e9s.","params":{"reason":"Raison de la r\u00e9vocation (pour la tra\u00e7abilit\u00e9)","tokenId":"Token \u00e0 r\u00e9voquer"}},"revokeRole(bytes32,address)":{"details":"Revokes `role` from `account`. If `account` had been granted `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event."},"royaltyInfo(uint256,uint256)":{"details":"Returns how much royalty is owed and to whom, based on a sale price that may be denominated in any unit of exchange. The royalty amount is denominated and should be paid in that same unit of exchange. NOTE: ERC-2981 allows setting the royalty to 100% of the price. In that case all the price would be sent to the royalty receiver and 0 tokens to the seller. Contracts dealing with royalty should consider empty transfers."},"safeTransferFrom(address,address,uint256)":{"details":"Safely transfers `tokenId` token from `from` to `to`, checking first that contract recipients are aware of the ERC-721 protocol to prevent tokens from being forever locked. Requirements: - `from` cannot be the zero address. - `to` cannot be the zero address. - `tokenId` token must exist and be owned by `from`. - If the caller is not `from`, it must have been allowed to move this token by either {approve} or   {setApprovalForAll}. - If `to` refers to a smart contract, it must implement {IERC721Receiver-onERC721Received}, which is called upon   a safe transfer. Emits a {Transfer} event."},"safeTransferFrom(address,address,uint256,bytes)":{"details":"Safely transfers `tokenId` token from `from` to `to`. Requirements: - `from` cannot be the zero address. - `to` cannot be the zero address. - `tokenId` token must exist and be owned by `from`. - If the caller is not `from`, it must be approved to move this token by either {approve} or {setApprovalForAll}. - If `to` refers to a smart contract, it must implement {IERC721Receiver-onERC721Received}, which is called upon   a safe transfer. Emits a {Transfer} event."},"setApprovalForAll(address,bool)":{"details":"Approve or remove `operator` as an operator for the caller. Operators can call {transferFrom} or {safeTransferFrom} for any token owned by the caller. Requirements: - The `operator` cannot be the address zero. Emits an {ApprovalForAll} event."},"setSoulboundContract(address)":{"details":"Appel\u00e9 apr\u00e8s le d\u00e9ploiement pour configurer l'architecture."},"setTokenRoyalty(uint256,address,uint96)":{"details":"Permet de configurer des royalties diff\u00e9rentes pour les \u00e9ditions limit\u00e9es."},"supportsInterface(bytes4)":{"details":"ERC165 : r\u00e9solution des conflits ERC721 / ERC2981 / AccessControl."},"symbol()":{"details":"Returns the token collection symbol."},"tokenURI(uint256)":{"details":"Returns the Uniform Resource Identifier (URI) for `tokenId` token."},"transferFrom(address,address,uint256)":{"details":"Transfers `tokenId` token from `from` to `to`. WARNING: Note that the caller is responsible to confirm that the recipient is capable of receiving ERC-721 or else they may be permanently lost. Usage of {safeTransferFrom} prevents loss, though the caller must understand this adds an external call which potentially creates a reentrancy vulnerability. Requirements: - `from` cannot be the zero address. - `to` cannot be the zero address. - `tokenId` token must be owned by `from`. - If the caller is not `from`, it must be approved to move this token by either {approve} or {setApprovalForAll}. Emits a {Transfer} event."}},"version":1},"userdoc":{"events":{"OwnershipTransferred(uint256,address,address,uint256)":{"notice":"\u00c9mis lors du transfert de propri\u00e9t\u00e9 approve(address,uint256).details: Gives permission to `to` to transfer `tokenId` token to another account. The approval is cleared when the token is transferred. Only a single account can be approved at a time, so approving the zero address clears previous approvals. Requirements: - The caller must own the token or be an approved operator. - `tokenId` must exist. Emits an {Approval} event.

balanceOf(address).details: Returns the number of tokens in ``owner``'s account.

params.royaltyFeeBps: Pourcentage de royalties en basis points (ex: 500 = 5%)

getApproved(uint256).details: Returns the account approved for `tokenId` token. Requirements: - `tokenId` must exist.

getRoleAdmin(bytes32).details: Returns the admin role that controls `role`. See {grantRole} and {revokeRole}. To change a role's admin, use {_setRoleAdmin}.

params.serialNumber: Numéro de série gravé sur la valise physique

grantRole(bytes32,address).details: Grants `role` to `account`. If `account` had not been already granted `role`, emits a {RoleGranted} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleGranted} event.

isApprovedForAll(address,address).details: Returns if the `operator` is allowed to manage all of the assets of `owner`. See {setApprovalForAll}

mintPassport(address,string,string).details: Seul un MINTER_ROLE peut appeler cette fonction.      Le numéro de série doit être unique — une valise = un token.      Le CID IPFS doit pointer vers les métadonnées JSON Pinata.      Aucune donnée brute n'est stockée ici, seulement le CID.

params.ipfsCid: CID IPFS du JSON de métadonnées (sans préfixe)
params.serialNumber: Numéro de série gravé sur la valise physique

ownerOf(uint256).details: Returns the owner of the `tokenId` token. Requirements: - `tokenId` must exist.

pause().details: Utilisé en cas de faille de sécurité détectée.

paused().details: Returns true if the contract is paused, and false otherwise.

renounceRole(bytes32,address).details: Revokes `role` from the calling account. Roles are often managed via {grantRole} and {revokeRole}: this function's purpose is to provide a mechanism for accounts to lose their privileges if they are compromised (such as when a trusted device is misplaced). If the calling account had been revoked `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must be `callerConfirmation`. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event.

revokePassport(uint256,string).details: Seul un REVOKER_ROLE peut révoquer. Le token reste dans le mapping      mais est marqué comme invalide. Les transferts sont bloqués.

params.reason: Raison de la révocation (pour la traçabilité)

revokeRole(bytes32,address).details: Revokes `role` from `account`. If `account` had been granted `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event.

royaltyInfo(uint256,uint256).details: Returns how much royalty is owed and to whom, based on a sale price that may be denominated in any unit of exchange. The royalty amount is denominated and should be paid in that same unit of exchange. NOTE: ERC-2981 allows setting the royalty to 100% of the price. In that case all the price would be sent to the royalty receiver and 0 tokens to the seller. Contracts dealing with royalty should consider empty transfers.

safeTransferFrom(address,address,uint256.details: Safely transfers `tokenId` token from `from` to `to`, checking first that contract recipients are aware of the ERC-721 protocol to prevent tokens from being forever locked. Requirements: - `from` cannot be the zero address. - `to` cannot be the zero address. - `tokenId` token must exist and be owned by `from`. - If the caller is not `from`, it must have been allowed to move this token by either {approve} or   {setApprovalForAll}. - If `to` refers to a smart contract, it must implement {IERC721Receiver-onERC721Received}, which is called upon   a safe transfer. Emits a {Transfer} event.

Safely transfers `tokenId` token from `from` to `to`. Requirements: - `from` cannot be the zero address. - `to` cannot be the zero address. - `tokenId` token must exist and be owned by `from`. - If the caller is not `from`, it must be approved to move this token by either {approve} or {setApprovalForAll}. - If `to` refers to a smart contract, it must implement {IERC721Receiver-onERC721Received}, which is called upon   a safe transfer. Emits a {Transfer} event.

setApprovalForAll(address,bool).details: Approve or remove `operator` as an operator for the caller. Operators can call {transferFrom} or {safeTransferFrom} for any token owned by the caller. Requirements: - The `operator` cannot be the address zero. Emits an {ApprovalForAll} event.

setSoulboundContract(address).details: Appelé après le déploiement pour configurer l'architecture.

setTokenRoyalty(uint256,address,uint96).details: Permet de configurer des royalties différentes pour les éditions limitées.

supportsInterface(bytes4).details: ERC165 : résolution des conflits ERC721 / ERC2981 / AccessControl.

tokenURI(uint256).details: Returns the Uniform Resource Identifier (URI) for `tokenId` token.

transferFrom(address,address,uint256).details: Transfers `tokenId` token from `from` to `to`. WARNING: Note that the caller is responsible to confirm that the recipient is capable of receiving ERC-721 or else they may be permanently lost. Usage of {safeTransferFrom} prevents loss, though the caller must understand this adds an external call which potentially creates a reentrancy vulnerability. Requirements: - `from` cannot be the zero address. - `to` cannot be the zero address. - `tokenId` token must be owned by `from`. - If the caller is not `from`, it must be approved to move this token by either {approve} or {setApprovalForAll}. Emits a {Transfer} event.

MINTER_ROLE().notice: MINTER_ROLE : accordé aux boutiques RIMOWA autorisées

PAUSER_ROLE().notice: PAUSER_ROLE : accordé à l'équipe de sécurité RIMOWA

REVOKER_ROLE().notice: REVOKER_ROLE : accordé à l'équipe anti-contrefaçon RIMOWA

getSerialByTokenId(uint256).notice: Retourne le numéro de série correspondant à un tokenId.

getTokenIdBySerial(string).notice: Retourne le tokenId correspondant à un numéro de série.

isPassportValid(uint256).notice: Vérifie si un passeport est valide (non révoqué et existant).

mintPassport(address,string,string).notice: Mint d'un nouveau passeport numérique RIMOWA.

pause().notice: Pause d'urgence — bloque tous les mints et transferts.

revokePassport(uint256,string).notice: Révoque un passeport en cas de contrefaçon prouvée ou défaut critique.

revoked(uint256).notice: Tokens révoqués (contrefaçon prouvée ou défaut critique)

setSoulboundContract(address).notice: Lie le contrat Soulbound (ERC-5192) à ce passeport.

setTokenRoyalty(uint256,address,uint96).notice: Définit des royalties spécifiques pour un token particulier.

soulboundContract().notice: Lien vers le contrat Soulbound associé

unpause().notice: Reprend les opérations après résolution de l'incident.
